title: Vegapunk Reveals the Sinking World
description: On pages 11–13, this part focuses on Unknown person #10, Unknown person #11, Marines, Unknown person #12, Chopper as Vegapunk’s message shakes the world by connecting the Void Century, Joy Boy, ancient weapons, the sinking world, and the Five Elders’ desperate attempt to stop the truth.
scene: Vegapunk’s message shakes the world by connecting the Void Century, Joy Boy, ancient weapons, the sinking world, and the Five Elders’ desperate attempt to stop the truth.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We all know travel between islands can be excruciatingly difficult.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The seas one must Traverse are so vast and perilous, most people are content to live and die at their place of birth, never experiencing any culture other than their own.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Isn't that a bit obvious?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Yeah, ha ha ha ha! Everyone knows the seas are dangerous.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: That is common knowledge to us now...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But if it wasn't like that 1000 years ago!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A cataclysmic ecological disaster occurred during the void century...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: And the ancient world was swallowed by the ocean!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: We are now living atop of the remnants,

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Continental fragments of the Land Masses that once were!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #10
dialogue.text: Say what?!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The world of a millennia ago sleeps, unseen, at the bottom of the ocean.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: I know it is hard to believe on my word alone...

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: But there really were multiple vast continents back then.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: This is insane!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey, NoJiko!! Where do you think you're going?!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: The whole world is at stake!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Fill me in later. I've gotta check on the tangerine orchard.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: NoJiko!!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: This change in the world helps explain how a century of history could be censored.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: A feat that seems nigh impossible, but it makes sense when you consider the undeniable conclusion all the data points to.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Within a mere century, the average sea level rose by a staggering...

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: ...200 meters!!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: What?! You're kidding!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #11
dialogue.text: T-T-Two hundred?!

dialogue.speaker: Marines
dialogue.text: A Warship's only 60 meters tall!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: No wonder the old world disappeared!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Hey, stop listening to this crap! We have work to do!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The Seraphim and all those imprisoned Cipher Pol agents were spotted falling out of the lab building!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: We've got to rescue them!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Did 'You hear that?! His tall tales keep getting taller!

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: The old fart's lost his marbles! What a joke!

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Sakura Kingdom (Chopper's hometown)

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: Is that really possible?

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh heh he

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: Even now the world still finds a way to keep things interesting.

dialogue.speaker: Unknown person #12
dialogue.text: That mountain is around 200 meters.

dialogue.speaker: Narrator
dialogue.text: You're saying something that tall would be submerged?! That's awful!! How many people died?!

summary: This part advances the Vegapunk’s Broadcast through 38 cleaned dialogue beats, centering the emotional stakes, battle movement, and the wider consequences of Egghead.
